CheckTitle: AI Agent Cross-Boundary Data Flows Must Be Approved and Documented
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Data Access**. Verifies that all data flows crossing trust boundaries (cross-account, cross-region, or to third-party systems) are explicitly whitelisted and documented in the agent manifest. Unapproved cross-boundary flows are a primary vector for data exfiltration.
Risk: Unapproved cross-boundary data flows can silently exfiltrate sensitive data to external systems or unauthorised accounts, violating data residency requirements and enabling supply-chain attacks.

Code.Other:
1. Enumerate all data flows that cross account, region, or organisational boundaries.
2. Obtain security and legal sign-off for each cross-boundary flow.
3. Document approved flows in the agent manifest and enforce them with egress firewall rules.

Recommendation.Text: Whitelist and document all cross-boundary data flows before deployment. Enforce approved flows via network policies and regularly review for new or undocumented flows.

Notes: ASPM-049 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
